speaker: narrator
text: By mid-morning the ironwoods began to thin. The air lost its tang of cold iron and sap; warm earth came up instead -- mud, wet moss, the faint yeasty breath of leaf-rot.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin walked center, the witch-knot ribbon Nyxara had tied at his wrist dull as old wine in the daylight. Every so often it ticked with the gentlest tug, as if a spider had plucked a single strand of web and let it sing.

speaker: narrator
text: Not a direction -- just a proof of distance, of attention. Someone, somewhere, still watching.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex ranged left, favoring her wrapped ribs but moving like a rumor. Durgan took the right, ice-blue eyes on the tree line, shadow obedient as a good dog at noon.

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric walked rear -- medical bag across one shoulder, eyes moving between the road ahead and the tree line Durgan was watching, doing his own quiet accounting of threats.

speaker: narrator
text: The world smelled almost domestic for the first time since the mountain -- woodsmoke drift from somewhere far downslope; a farm's latrine, honest and rank. Civilization as scent.

speaker: narrator
text: They hit the ruts of the old Merchant Road by noon -- two pale crescents pressed into clay, puddles winking with sky. Ahead, a broken cart tilted against a ditch. A kettle still hung from its hook, long gone cold. Chicken feathers lay stuck to sap on a fallen branch. Someone had left in a hurry.

speaker: vex
text: "Tracks,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, crouching.

speaker: vex
text: "Two families. One cartwheel cracked, they ditched it. Southbound -- same as us."

speaker: narrator
text: She sniffed the air, a feral, automatic habit.

speaker: vex
text: "Yesterday, maybe sunrise."

speaker: narrator
text: The ribbon on Caelin's wrist gave that whisper-tug again. He didn't look at it.

speaker: narrator
text: He touched the bronzed ward-disc Serana had pressed into his palm when she left -- the surface etched with a line of secular intention, protective not because a god blessed it, but because a stubborn woman had. The metal held a little warmth.

speaker: narrator
text: It steadied him more than he liked admitting.

speaker: durgan
text: "Company,"

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan murmured.

speaker: narrator
text: They saw the woman first -- a farmer, hair gone wild, dress torn at the hem, one arm around a boy not more than seven and the other around a bundle that might have been bread or a baby.

speaker: narrator
text: She stood in the road's center, frozen like a doe, staring at the black smear moving along the ditch shadow -- a scout's shade, thin as spilled ink.

speaker: narrator
text: The thing reached for the boy's feet.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan moved.

speaker: narrator
text: He didn't think. He went.

speaker: narrator
text: One clean sprint that ate the distance, one boot heel that slammed the shadow flat against bright clay, and then the soldier's rhythm took over -- draw, slash, step; blade in a short arc that cut where light and ground met, the place shadows anchor.

speaker: narrator
text: The shade recoiled, hissing without a mouth, and shivered itself thin to slither away.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan planted a second cut that pinned it like a nail through silk.

speaker: vex
text: "Run,"

speaker: narrator
text: he told the woman without looking back.

speaker: durgan
text: "Don't stop until you see a palisade."

speaker: narrator
text: She didn't argue. The boy stared at Durgan's face like he'd never seen a man before; then he was swept down the road by his mother's panic.

speaker: aldric
text: "Check the boy for shock when you stop!"

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric called after them.

speaker: durgan
text: "He'll be quiet -- that's the wrong kind of quiet!"

speaker: narrator
text: He watched them go, then looked at the smear on the road where the shade had been.

speaker: aldric
text: "That's different from what came through the vault."

speaker: narrator
text: He said it without emphasis, matter-of-fact.

speaker: durgan
text: "The Veln's Post thing was bigger. Older. This is a forward scout."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's eyes flicked to Durgan when it was done. Approval was not her currency, but it showed anyway, quick as light on a blade.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin exhaled a breath he hadn't known he held. The scale in his forearm said nothing -- no sting, no warning. The world smelled of crushed grass and the metallic, coin-thin scent the shade had left behind.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan's nose was dry when he wiped it with the back of his hand. He checked anyway. Habit. Waiting.

speaker: caelin
text: "Thanks,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan sheathed his blade.

speaker: durgan
text: "Keep moving."

speaker: narrator
text: They did.

speaker: narrator
text: They smelled tar before they saw the barricade.

speaker: narrator
text: The road bent between two low knuckles of granite where ironwoods had failed to root; smoke lay in the turn like old breath. Four horses stood side by side, heads down, lather drying white on their necks.

speaker: narrator
text: A rope-drag of dead brush spanned the road, glistening with black pitch. Six men in leather and scale waited behind it, crossbows already braced on the rocks.

speaker: narrator
text: One wore a red kerchief at his throat; one had the lazy stance of someone who'd killed often enough to be bored by it.

speaker: caelin
text: "Afternoon,"

speaker: narrator
text: the bored one said.

speaker: durgan
text: "You match a description."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's mouth twitched.

speaker: caelin
text: "Let me guess. 'Tall, cursed, and bad at avoiding trouble.'"

speaker: durgan
text: "Something like that."

speaker: narrator
text: The bored man's gaze never left Caelin's arm.

speaker: caelin
text: "Bounty says alive. Your friends may sulk off or not. Choice is a luxury but I'm magnanimous."

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric, standing at the back of the group, set his medical bag down on the road with the quiet deliberateness of a man who has been in enough of these situations to know that the bag stays where it can be reached quickly. He folded his hands and said nothing. He was not going to sulk off.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin's scale warmed. Not a flare; a gathering. He counted breaths, counted costs. The pitch-brush smelled like shipyards and siege ladders. Tar's flash point lived in his hands if he asked for it.

speaker: vex
text: "Crossbow pivots,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex murmured from the corner of her mouth.

speaker: caelin
text: "Two right, one left, leader center. Drop center, I delete right. You still standing?"

speaker: caelin
text: "Standing,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said. He flexed his hand. The lattice tugged back, a warning twine, but his body had learned new shapes for pain.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan's voice stayed flat.

speaker: vex
text: "Last chance. Walk away."

speaker: narrator
text: The red-kerchief man smiled with his eyes.

speaker: caelin
text: "No."

speaker: narrator
text: Time folded.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex moved first. A silver line crossed sunlight -- her throwing knife -- and the central bowman jerked as if a wasp stung his eye, then folded to the dust.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin breathed in. Heat gathered behind his ribs the way a forge gathers rightness; he pictured not fire but temperature, a widow's kiss that rushed through air without flame. His palm lifted.

speaker: narrator
text: The pitch line sighed, then whoomph -- tar flared in a low, fast curtain. Black smoke stamped the sky. The barricade twisted and fell.

speaker: narrator
text: Bolts sang toward them anyway -- two, three. One hissed past Caelin's ear. One struck stone and cracked like a promise. One took Durgan's sleeve and left a slice of red without slowing him.

speaker: narrator
text: He was already in their faces, blades walking the short economy of a soldier trained for close work. Two men went down with groans that would fade or not; he left them breathing on purpose.

speaker: narrator
text: Pain pealed through Caelin's arm -- more bell than blade. He took it. Let it ring his bones. He dropped to one knee and pulled air through his teeth, felt the lattice complain, then settle into the old, ugly baseline.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex hit the right-hand pair like consequence. First man's wrist -- snapped; second's kneecap -- cut; she drove her pommel into a temple and let him sleep out his poor choices.

speaker: narrator
text: The last bounty hunter -- the bored one -- dropped his crossbow, raised his hands, and smiled again with nothing to smile about.

speaker: vex
text: "Mercy?"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan's knife was at his throat.

speaker: caelin
text: "You'll leave a message."

speaker: vex
text: "Ah. To whom?"

speaker: caelin
text: "Whoever pays you. Tell them you saw the fire and lived. Tell them next time you won't."

speaker: narrator
text: The man swallowed, his Adam's apple pushing the blade.

speaker: vex
text: "I can do polite."

speaker: caelin
text: "Do it farther away."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan stepped back. The shadow under his boots stayed ordinary in the full hammer of noon.

speaker: narrator
text: They dragged the pitch brush off the road with their boots. The world was tar reek and burned horsehair and the sour tang of someone's fear-sweat.

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric went straight to Durgan.

speaker: vex
text: "Sleeve up."

speaker: caelin
text: "It's a scratch."

speaker: vex
text: "Sleeve up."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan pushed up the sleeve. The bolt had opened a four-inch line across the forearm -- clean, not deep. Aldric cleaned it with something from a small flask that smelled medicinal and unpleasant, bound it with three quick turns of linen, and tied it off without ceremony.

speaker: caelin
text: "Keep it dry. It won't."

speaker: narrator
text: He looked at Caelin on one knee.

speaker: vex
text: "How's the arm?"

speaker: caelin
text: "The same."

speaker: vex
text: "That's not an answer."

speaker: caelin
text: "It's the answer I have."

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric looked at him for a moment with the expression of a man who'd heard that particular response in that particular tone before, from men who were about to find out it wasn't enough. He let it go. For now.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex wiped her knife on a patch of clean grass and cocked a brow at Caelin.

speaker: vex
text: "How many of those,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, nodding at the blackened barricade,

speaker: caelin
text: "before you fall down and I have to carry you?"

speaker: caelin
text: "I'm not sure,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin admitted.

speaker: vex
text: "Fewer than we'd like. More than yesterday."

speaker: caelin
text: "Progress,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, and almost smiled.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan wiped his sleeve. No blood on his hand this time. He exhaled once, like a man setting a weight down where no one would notice.

speaker: narrator
text: They took the horses nobody wanted to keep and rode until the light went copper.

speaker: narrator
text: Evening found them on the lip of an old wound -- an earthquake's mouth split months ago or centuries, who could say. A basalt bridge arched the gap in a single impossible span, black as a raven's back, edges beveled to a craftsman's vanity.

speaker: narrator
text: Wind lived down there. It smelled of cold stone and something old and salty, though the sea lay weeks away.

speaker: narrator
text: At the near end of the bridge, someone had carved a sigil into a waist-high stone: a circle doubled back on itself, nine spokes, the tenth line severed. Thornik would have told them the lineage of the chisel marks.

speaker: narrator
text: In his absence, Caelin only felt seen. The scale warmed, shyly. Vex touched the symbol with two fingers and let the gesture be a greeting.

speaker: narrator
text: Halfway across, the light changed.

speaker: narrator
text: Not dimmer -- stranger. The sun still bled out across the world, but the space over the span held a second dusk of its own, a hush laid over air. Caelin tasted metal on his tongue. The ribbon at his wrist gave a single bright pluck, like a harpstring in a room with no hands.

speaker: narrator
text: Words raised from the stone, not carved but standing -- letters the eye only loved if it looked from the side.

speaker: narrator
text: Speak true or speak not at all.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex stopped dead.

speaker: vex
text: "I hate bridges that talk."

speaker: caelin
text: "It's a ward,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said. He took out Serana's disc. The secular line etched into bronze caught the odd light and answered in a soft, stubborn glow. The sense of it wasn't holy, not anymore. It was made strong by work. By intention. I will keep what I can.

speaker: narrator
text: He cleared his throat.

speaker: vex
text: "We are crossing to the plateau. We intend to harm no one who does not try to harm us. We seek a prison that isn't a prison."

speaker: narrator
text: He paused. Laughed once at himself.

speaker: caelin
text: "We're in over our heads."

speaker: narrator
text: The bridge listened. Said nothing. But the pressure eased enough to breathe.

speaker: vex
text: "Fine,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, and stepped into the ward's hush. She could have lied to it with panache. She chose not to.

speaker: caelin
text: "I am very tired,"

speaker: narrator
text: she informed the air.

speaker: vex
text: "I am very hungry. I do not want to die before I stab whoever designed this road."

speaker: narrator
text: The hush let her pass.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan hesitated a fraction. His shadow bled one inch toward the parapet, stopped as if it had hit glass, and recoiled like a burned animal. The smell of singed wool flicked the air then died, leaving the clean ache of wind.

speaker: caelin
text: "I will not hurt them,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said to no one and everyone.

speaker: vex
text: "Not while I am me."

speaker: narrator
text: The ward accepted that as truth.

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric stepped up to the ward's edge and stood there a moment with his hands at his sides. He didn't look like a man performing sincerity. He looked like a man who'd decided what was true and was simply reporting it.

speaker: caelin
text: "I came to find out what killed thirty-seven men,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "And if there's anything left to do about it."

speaker: narrator
text: The hush considered this and let him through.

speaker: narrator
text: They stood at the bridge's crown and looked north. The world beyond rolled away in bruised gold: scrub, broken stone, low thorn.

speaker: narrator
text: And there, like a nail driven into the horizon, lay a black plateau shouldering out of the world -- the Depthspire rise, not yet the thing itself, but the country of it.

speaker: narrator
text: Above, a pale aurora wrote thin curtains across the sky where no such lights should run.

speaker: narrator
text: A square of folded paper fell out of Caelin's pack and landed against his boot.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's hand hit a dagger before he'd bent to pick it up. Durgan's blades whispered half-free and went still when nothing moved to justify it.

speaker: narrator
text: The note was ordinary to the point of insolence -- cheap stock, black ink, a smell of old lavender as if it had slept too long in an honest drawer.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin opened it.

speaker: narrator
text: Your pace is sufficient. Keep the road's left edge when the basalt goes to clay; the right is mined by men who will not remember why they laid the charges. I will not wait long at the first watch-tower. -- L

speaker: narrator
text: Vex made a face that belonged to someone who'd bitten a green apple.

speaker: caelin
text: "Divination and manners,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: vex
text: "Pick one."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin folded the note, slid it away.

speaker: caelin
text: "He's ahead of us."

speaker: vex
text: "Or behind and being clever about it."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex shaded her eyes against a wind that didn't need shading.

speaker: caelin
text: "Either way, I vote we don't sleep on the bridge."

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric looked at the note Caelin had pocketed, then at the plateau on the horizon, then said nothing. He'd stopped asking who L was. The answer was somewhere north of them and he'd find out when they got there.

speaker: narrator
text: They finished the span, and the hush let them go. On the far side, someone had piled three stones into a cairn you'd miss if you blinked.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin set Serana's disc on the top for a breath, warmed it with the heat the scale made when it was patient, and took it back. The night smelled of sage crushed under boots and basalt cooling after day.

speaker: narrator
text: They made camp in the lee of a boulder where the wind could sing without tearing the blankets loose. The ribbon at Caelin's wrist lay quiet. The ward-disc held its stubborn warmth.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan set himself where he could see both the bridge and their fire; when his nose bled, he turned his head and let the dark drink it.

speaker: narrator
text: Aldric sat with his back to the boulder and his bag between his feet and looked north, doing whatever arithmetic he'd been doing since Veln's Post and not sharing the result.

speaker: narrator
text: Sleep came as a courtesy, not a gift.
